mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate wage income, which drives consumption and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month change and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% imposes a restrictive discount rate on equity cash flows and raises the probability of overtightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value 4.57% as of Feb 2023
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment and elevated consumer price inflation tighten financial conditions through a stagflationary channel that damages earnings visibility more than either variable alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months while CPIAUCSL exceeds 300
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value 301.648 and UNRATE 3.6 as of Feb 2023
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of triggered months, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression does not significantly improve R-squared (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts from a 12-month peak signal the end of residential investment momentum and foreshadow broader demand deceleration, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% below its trailing 12-month maximum
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value 1309 as of Jan 2023
failure_condition: HOUST drawdowns from 12-month peaks coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during economic expansions.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production over a 3-month window reflects rising real output and capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate revenue surprises and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 3-month INDPRO momentum coincides with negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 45% of triggered weeks over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in retail sales exceeding 2% signals a sudden collapse in consumer spending that reduces corporate revenue forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS contractions greater than 2% coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.0% that is still falling indicates a tight labor market supporting wage growth and consumption without triggering recession alarms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.0% and declines over prior month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value 3.6 as of Feb 2023
failure_condition: The trigger condition coincides with negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 45% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above its 2-year trend indicates aggregate demand running above potential, which supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds trailing 8-quarter average
failure_condition: Above-trend GDPC1 growth precedes negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 45% of triggered quarters.
